summary: Case delta for the depts module is complete and ready for downstream API planning. 14 ADDED cases cover all five backend endpoints (list, get, create, update, delete) with happy-path, negative, and edge cases (closure rebuild, unique-name constraint, dept_id parameter name, auth boundary). All cases satisfy the four-layer rule (single source of truth in type=API, framework=pytest), use natural-language steps and observable assertions, and reference existing fixtures from .aws/data-knowledge.yaml. E2E scope deliberately deferred per runtime test_types=api (overriding .qa.yaml default), documented in proposal.md Out-of-Scope and Layer Rationale sections.

findings.message: Five P1 cases (TC-DEPT-API-002/004/007/011/013) carry risk.level=medium rather than high/critical, which deviates from the default Priority↔Risk consistency guidance.
findings.suggestion: No action — the mismatch is intentional and explicitly documented in proposal.md under 'Priority / Risk-Level Mismatch (Documented)'. Each is a negative case whose impact is bounded by the loose assertion (non-5xx + controlled error), keeping likelihood×impact in the medium band.

findings.message: TC-DEPT-API-014 (auth boundary) currently exercises only the list endpoint without a token; the other four endpoints' auth behaviour is asserted by inheritance (FastAPI router-level DependPermission) rather than by direct case coverage.
findings.suggestion: Acceptable for this baseline: the FastAPI router mounts all dept endpoints under a single DependPermission dependency (verified in app/api/v1/__init__.py), so testing one endpoint is a sound representative sample. If the team later splits the dependency per-endpoint, add cases for the remaining four endpoints.

delta_operation_validation.result: pass — target file does not exist, all 14 cases correctly placed in 'added'
